label: Australian construction cost benchmarks by building and infrastructure type
caveat: Indicative benchmarks from Rawlinsons Construction Cost Guide 2026 and publicly available QS sources. NOT substitutes for quantity surveyor estimates. State and regional variation is significant (apply location factors). Costs exclude external works, professional fees, land, GST, and furniture/equipment unless noted.

primary_school.note: Per student cost: AUD 14,000-30,000 depending on city and specification

hospital_general.note: General hospital. Operating theatres, imaging suites, and specialist clinical areas at the upper end.

social_housing.note: State housing authorities typically target AUD 300k-450k per dwelling (1-2 bed unit).

motorway_urban_per_km.note: Enormous range. At-grade rural highway at the low end. Urban motorway tunnels (WestConnex, NorthConnex) at the upper end.

rail_metro_per_km.source: Melbourne Metro Tunnel, Sydney Metro data

light_rail_per_km.source: Gold Coast Light Rail, Canberra Light Rail, Sydney Light Rail data

level_crossing_removal.note: 85 crossings being removed. Average cost ~AUD 200-250m per crossing. Includes road/rail grade separation, station rebuild, and precinct development.

location_factors.description: City-level cost indices relative to Sydney (1.00). Based on Rawlinsons and QS industry data. Regional and remote areas have significant premiums due to labour availability and transport costs.
location_factors.source: Rawlinsons 2026 location factors, industry QS benchmarks
location_factors.note: Remote and regional areas are MORE expensive than capital cities due to labour shortages, accommodation costs, and materials transport. The Pilbara premium (1.40x) reflects FIFO workforce costs.

additions_pct.description: Common additions on top of base sqm rates.

professional_fees.note: Typically lower fee percentages than UK due to different market structure

gst.note: GST applies to construction. Government entities can generally claim GST credits. Private sector: GST is additional to stated costs.

additions_pct.escalation: Apply construction cost escalation for projects with long lead times. Australian construction cost inflation has been volatile (5-10% p.a. in 2022-24, moderating to 3-5% in 2025).

uk_to_au_comparison.description: Rough conversion factors for comparing UK and AU construction costs. These are indicative only.
uk_to_au_comparison.exchange_rate_note: At AUD 1.00 = GBP 0.50 (mid-2025), Australian construction costs appear similar to or slightly above UK costs in GBP terms. However, purchasing power parity suggests Australian costs are genuinely 10-20% higher than UK for comparable buildings.
key_differences: Australian buildings typically have higher seismic and wind load specifications than UK
Energy efficiency requirements differ (NCC vs Building Regs)
Labour costs are higher in Australia (minimum wage AUD 24.10/hr vs UK GBP 11.44/hr)
Materials costs are comparable, but transport to regional/remote sites adds significantly in Australia
Australian construction has lower productivity than UK (OECD data)

publications: Rawlinsons Construction Cost Guide 2026 (January 2026 base rates)
AIQS (Australian Institute of Quantity Surveyors) benchmarks
source.note: Rawlinsons is proprietary (AUD 250-400). Values above are from publicly available QS sources, government publications, and industry reports. For project-level estimates, purchase the Rawlinsons guide or commission a QS.
